sports_feed._note: Refreshed by the refresh-sports-feed scheduled task. Each item is {headline, url, source, published (ISO8601), snark}. Snark voice: Barstool-adjacent guy-at-the-bar, crass, horny, cocky, zero filler. One sentence. No slurs, no punching down at specific real people in a harmful way. Punch sideways (at media, front offices, rival fanbases) and up (at ownership). Keep it Boston.

items.headline: Red Sox at Yankees preview: Sonny Gray gets the ball in Bronx
items.snark: Last place Sox, 10.5 back, facing the Yanks without Judge feels like showing up to a knife fight with a spork.

items.headline: Sox drop series finale 8-2 to Orioles, now 10-21 at Fenway Park
items.snark: Red Sox home record is the worst in baseball and somehow still not the most embarrassing thing happening at Fenway.

items.headline: In sad Sox spring of 2026, they're no longer the talk of the town
items.snark: Shaughnessy says Sox fans would rather talk about A.J. Brown than their own team. Brutal and accurate.

items.headline: Brayan Bello optioned to Triple-A Worcester after giving up 6 first-inning runs
items.snark: Bello's first-inning ERA is 16.88 as a starter, 0.71 as a reliever, which is the baseball version of stage fright.

items.headline: Sox pitching prospects rack up 14 Ks in 14.3 scoreless innings Tuesday
items.snark: Farm system is absolutely dealing while the big club can't score enough runs to beat a college squad.

items.headline: Isiah Kiner-Falefa says there's a different vibe at Fenway, won't elaborate
items.snark: IKF complaining about too many people around the clubhouse like a teenager mad his parents came home early.

items.headline: Patriots preseason finale at Browns moved to Prime Video national broadcast
items.snark: Amazon paying real money to broadcast third-stringers in late August. Content is truly king.

ranked_opportunities.scqa: The SAR Buyer Side
ranked_opportunities.verdict: EnduroSat is a Bulgarian software-flexible satellite manufacturer at a US-build inflection: $147M raised in six months (Founders Fund, Riot, Google Ventures), new EnduroSat USA CEO Rusty Thomas (former DARPA Project Blackjack) tripling Denver from 5 to 15+. The March 2026 MetaSensing X-band SAR partnership (PhoeniX, StarSAR-X on FRAME, ~0.125m resolution) is the most commercially interesting move and a direct match to Paul's KSAT decade selling NRT SAR services into oil and gas, maritime, and environmental compliance. Tier 2, pursue with caveats; the early-conversation question is whether this seat is commercial-services-led or pulled toward the DC gov-pipeline build (Shield Space, SDA-supplier outreach).
ranked_opportunities.doc_note: Full deep dive completed 2026-05-15. Top SCQA: The SAR Buyer Side (91.5/100). Recommended resume: EO/Maritime (primary), Ecosystem/Platform (alternate). Open commercial-vs-gov scoping question with Rusty Thomas before applying.

ranked_opportunities.hook: MetaSensing X-band SAR-as-a-service offering on FRAME platform is a direct match to Paul's KSAT SAR services decade. First BDM in a 5-to-15 Denver build under an ex-DARPA CEO with $147M US capital pressure.

blockers.detail: JD states 'primarily on-site and in person at the office' in Denver, CO; Paul is in Taos, NM. Soft blocker, Denver is the easiest US aerospace city for Paul to relocate to (short flight from ABQ, Mountain West).

ranked_opportunities.role: Senior Director, Subscription & Data Solutions @ LevelTen Energy | Techstars Job Board
ranked_opportunities.verdict: This role requires deep domain experience in the renewable energy PPA market that Paul does not possess. The JD explicitly requires "10+ years of experience in the renewable energy market, with deep knowledge of PPAs, energy procurement, and the buyer/seller/advisor/developer ecosystem" and "Significant commercial or sales experience within this market, you have personally sold to or managed relationships with energy buyers, developers, utilities, or advisors." Paul's 15 years of commercial infrastructure experience is in satellite monitoring (oil & gas, fishing surveillance, maritime), not renewable energy transactions. While the role structure, commercial-product bridge, subscription packaging, data monetization strategy, maps beautifully to Paul's skills from GFW (building commercial licensing for an open platform, multi-stakeholder deal architecture), the sector mismatch is a hard blocker. RF capped at 5 because the responsibilities fit Paul's profile but the required domain does not. IL capped at 4 because Paul cannot credibly claim the named renewable energy market experience or customer base. The role is well-compensated and the stage is interesting (post-PMF data platform scaling subscriptions), but Paul would need to explain a full sector pivot from space/maritime to renewable energy, and the JD signals they want someone who has "been living and breathing this market for years."

blockers.type: Hard requirement: 10+ years renewable energy market experience
blockers.detail: JD requires '10+ years of experience in the renewable energy market, with deep knowledge of PPAs, energy procurement, and the buyer/seller/advisor/developer ecosystem.' Paul has 15 years in commercial space/satellite sector (KSAT 2011-2021, GFW 2021-2026) but not in renewable energy PPAs, energy procurement, or the utility/developer ecosystem that LevelTen serves. His infrastructure work was water/wastewater utility security (PA Consulting) and satellite monitoring, not renewable energy transactions.

blockers.type: Hard requirement: Significant commercial/sales experience selling to energy buyers, developers, utilities, advisors
blockers.detail: JD requires 'Significant commercial or sales experience within this market, you have personally sold to or managed relationships with energy buyers, developers, utilities, or advisors and understand how deals get done.' Paul's sales experience is in satellite monitoring services to oil & gas, fishing surveillance, and maritime clients. He has not sold to renewable energy buyers, PPAs, or clean energy developers.

ranked_opportunities.verdict: Fortune 500 agribusiness seeking to commercialize carbon credits from its CCUS assets across U.S. and international markets. The role itself maps cleanly to Paul's market-builder pattern (build new customers and pathways for a novel commodity, operate in fast-evolving market with incomplete information, CBAM exposure). But JD requires 10+ years in commodity trading or merchandising, which is a domain Paul has not worked in, and the role is explicitly on-site in Chicago or Decatur (#LI-Onsite). ADM is also an ag conglomerate rather than a mission-driven climate venture, which is outside Paul's stated target profile. Pass unless Paul wants to use this as a carbon-market learning conversation.

ranked_opportunities.verdict: This is a carbon credit sales role at an Asia-focused carbon project platform. While the mission aligns strongly with Paul's climate/sustainability focus and the entrepreneurial framing is appealing, the core work is enterprise carbon credit sales ,  managing pipeline, closing deals, forecasting ,  not building a commercial function or creating demand in a new market. The JD explicitly requires "7-10 years of experience in enterprise sales, business development, strategic account management" and describes the role as "responsible for the entire carbon credits sales cycle." Paul's KSAT and GFW experience demonstrates commercial development and multi-stakeholder deal architecture, but he is not a quota-carrying enterprise seller. RF capped at 5 because the role shape does not match Paul's market-builder identity. IL at 5 because Paul has relevant carbon/climate sector knowledge (GFW commercial licensing, KSAT EO for environmental compliance) but lacks the named "carbon market sales experience strongly preferred." SA scores 6 because Rubicon is described as expanding and the role is "newly added due to business expansion," but the role itself is about selling an existing product (carbon credits) to enterprises, not building the commercial function. SPM scores 7 because multi-stakeholder carbon credit deals and navigating complex corporate buyers fits Paul's pattern, even though the role itself is misaligned. Singapore preference is a soft geographic blocker.

blockers.detail: Role has 'strong preference for candidates based in Singapore.' Paul is based in Taos, NM. While location is described as 'flexible,' the preference signal creates friction.
Role is 'entire carbon credits sales cycle' including 'managing pipeline, executing account strategies' ,  this is quota-carrying enterprise sales, not market-building.

ranked_opportunities.scqa: Interconnection Queue is the Real Moat
ranked_opportunities.verdict: Rescored 2026-04-22 after Bentley-style inflation caught. JD explicitly requires '12+ years in energy development, power markets, energy-tech commercialization, or infrastructure strategy.' Paul has none of the four in the way Crusoe means them, which caps RF at 4 and IL at 3 under the Hard Requirement Cap rule. The role language sounds like Paul's pattern (pilot-to-precedent, multi-stakeholder deal architecture) but Crusoe's work is selling to ISOs, RTOs, IPPs, and financiers in power-market tariff land, which is a different profession. SPM stays high (7) because the pattern IS real, but per the firewall rule it does not rescue RF. Recommendation: pass. Deep dive remains in Desktop folder for reference but should not be used for application.

ranked_opportunities.verdict: This role requires a Technical Product Manager with 5+ years managing software platform development, working directly with engineering teams on backend systems, data pipelines, and platform architecture. Paul has deep domain expertise in satellite data platforms (KSAT, GFW), strong stakeholder coordination across government and commercial buyers, and demonstrated ability to translate complex requirements into pilot outcomes—but his career has been commercial/market development, not internal software product management. The JD explicitly requires someone who can "translate product requirements into clear technical specifications, data models, and platform capabilities" and "work closely with engineering teams on backend systems, data pipelines, or platform architecture." Paul's experience is adjacent but not on-role. RF capped at 5 (sector fit strong, role fit weak); IL capped at 4 (no PM title or credential on paper). SA scores 6 because Axelspace is scaling a platform but the role itself is product infrastructure, not market-building. SPM scores 6 for multi-stakeholder coordination fit, but the three patterns don't directly apply to internal platform PM work. This is a "right domain, wrong chair" scenario.

blockers.detail: JD requires '5+ years of experience in software product management focused on B2B/SaaS platforms.' Paul has 15 years building commercial programs for EO/satellite data but as a commercial/market development role, not as a software product manager. His KSAT and GFW work involved defining data platform requirements from the customer side and shaping product roadmaps through pilot feedback, but he was not the internal PM owning the engineering backlog.

JD requires 'Experience working closely with engineering teams on backend systems, data pipelines, or platform architecture.' Paul has worked with engineering teams to define requirements and validate outputs, but his role was commercial/BD, not embedded product management with direct responsibility for technical specifications, data models, or platform architecture decisions.

ranked_opportunities.verdict: This role fails on three hard constraints. First, geographic: Singapore-based with strong preference for local candidates; Paul is in Taos and Singapore relocation is a substantial ask. Second, the JD explicitly requires "10, 15+ years of relevant client-facing deal making experience with 5-7 years+ in carbon markets, climate finance, project investing" ,  Paul has 5 years total in the climate/carbon space (GFW 2021-2026), not 5-7+ in carbon markets specifically. Third, the role demands "deep expertise in carbon markets, including project pathways, quality guardrails, offtake structures, supply and demand dynamics" and "significant experience originating, structuring, negotiating, and executing complex multi-million dollar commercial transactions, with a focus on environmental commodity transactions." Paul built a commercial licensing program at GFW; he did not invest in carbon projects, structure offtakes, or negotiate carbon credit purchase agreements. RF capped at 5 (role involves deal-making and multi-stakeholder coordination but in a domain Paul lacks depth), IL capped at 4 (experience gap is explicit and quantified), GC capped at 2 (Singapore residency preference is explicit). SPM scores 8 because the multi-stakeholder deal architecture, portfolio construction, and market-building patterns are strongly present, but that does not overcome the hard blockers.

blockers.detail: Role based in Singapore with strong preference for Singapore-based candidates. Paul is based in Taos, NM and while open to relocation, Singapore represents a significant geographic and cultural distance.

Requires 10-15+ years relevant client-facing deal making experience with 5-7 years+ specifically in carbon markets, climate finance, project investing, infrastructure, commodities, or structured finance. Paul has 5 years at GFW (2021-2026) in carbon/climate but lacks the requisite depth in carbon project investment, carbon credit offtakes, or carbon market structuring.

Requires deep expertise in carbon markets including project pathways, quality guardrails, offtake structures, supply and demand dynamics, delivery and issuance risk. Paul's carbon market experience is limited to building GFW's commercial licensing program, not investing in or structuring carbon credit projects.

ranked_opportunities.verdict: This role requires Singapore residency with Asia-based travel (hard geographic blocker) and 5-7+ years of direct carbon market investment experience that Paul does not have. The JD explicitly requires "Deep expertise in carbon markets, including project pathways, quality guardrails, offtake structures, supply and demand dynamics, and key diligence considerations such as delivery and issuance risk" and "10, 15+ years of relevant client-facing deal making experience with 5-7 years+ in carbon markets." Paul's GFW commercial licensing work and KSAT commercial development demonstrate strong pattern alignment (multi-stakeholder deals, innovative financing structures, new market building) earning SPM 8, but his carbon market exposure is as an adjacent player, not a carbon project investor. RF capped at 5 because the role is structured around carbon project investment mechanics Paul hasn't practiced; IL capped at 4 on experience requirements; GC capped at 2 on Singapore residency.

The earlier "Ports" claim (via KSAT maritime and GFW coastal) confused maritime domain awareness with port-authority infrastructure selling, which Bentley actually targets. The strategic pattern match (SPM 6) is real, Muon is at a 'zero-to-one' commercial inflection and the role involves customer discovery and translating operational needs into product concepts, but the actual job is wrong for Paul's skill set and he does not meet the hard technical and clearance requirements.

ranked_opportunities.verdict: This is a tactical project management role focused on coordinating EM system deployments, installations, and operational logistics, not commercial market-building. The JD explicitly describes the work as "day-to-day project coordination," "track deployment schedules," and "coordinate installation and maintenance campaigns across ports." Paul's career has been about creating commercial demand and building markets; this role is about executing existing monitoring programs. The CAD 75k-90k range places it at junior-to-mid PM level, well below Paul's seniority. Victoria, BC location with structured in-office culture adds geographic friction. While Archipelago operates in Paul's fisheries monitoring domain and the SPM score reflects genuine pattern overlap (multi-stakeholder coordination in regulated fisheries), the role itself is operationally focused execution, not the commercial market-building work Paul does best.

ranked_opportunities.verdict: This role is a hard pass due to three unmet requirements that trigger caps. First, the JD explicitly requires "5 years in B2B sales or a commercial client-facing role; at least 1 year carrying a quota" and states "You will carry your own quota." Paul has never carried a traditional quota—his 15-year career has been market-building and strategic BD, not quota-attainment AE work. Second, the JD requires "Strong written and verbal communication skills in both Spanish and English"; there is no evidence Paul speaks Spanish. Third, the role requires "demonstrable ability to run a structured sales process" in the context of mid-market quota-carrying AE work; Paul's documented pattern is pilot-to-precedent regulatory market creation, not transactional quota attainment. RF caps at 3 because the role definition fundamentally mismatches Paul's market-builder identity. The mission (climate-risk analytics for infrastructure) is a strong SPM and sector fit, but the role itself is wrong.

applications.verdict: VP-level role at a Series B critical-minerals scale-up (Phoenix Tailings, Burlington MA, $116M raised, BMW/Yamaha/Eni/Traxys backed). Excellent SPM (post-PMF commercial scaling, multi-year strategic offtake architecture, C-suite partnership work) and excellent SA (Series B with real revenue + strategic investor alignment). RF is 6 because rare-earth metallurgy is not Paul's domain, but the JD weights commercial pattern over domain depth at this level. Two specific frictions: (a) Paul applied for Director of BD on Mar 25 and was rejected same day with note encouraging future applications, (b) Burlington on-site requirement vs Taos. Both addressable; the rejection actually argues FOR this role being a better shape than the Director one was.
